text: Philosophers distinguish two kinds of body knowledge: the objective body (Körper in German) studied by anatomy, and the lived body (Leib) experienced from within. Merleau-Ponty insisted phenomenology must begin from the Leib, because only through lived bodily experience does the world acquire meaning. A carpenter does not consciously calculate a hammer's weight — the tool becomes an extension of the body schema, felt through the nail rather than in the hand. This incorporation of objects is not metaphorical; neuroimaging studies confirm that tool use reshapes the neural representation of the arm's peripersonal space within minutes.
question: What do neuroimaging studies reveal about tool use, according to the passage?

options.text: Tool use gradually replaces proprioceptive signals from the arm over several weeks
Tool use activates memory regions associated with prior manual training
Tool use suppresses objective body feedback to allow concentrated focus
Tool use reshapes the neural representation of the arm's peripersonal space within minutes
